commit.message:
ci: add checks:write permission for test-reporter

dorny/test-reporter@v1 requires checks:write permission to create
check runs. Without it, the action fails with 'Resource not accessible
by integration' on pull requests.

feat!: inline plugin skills config, workspace schema v2 (#233) (#236)

BREAKING CHANGE: skill selection is now configured inline on each
plugin entry instead of top-level disabledSkills/enabledSkills arrays.

- Simplify plugin-skills CLI: remove inAllowlistMode detection, use SkillInfo.pluginSkillsMode
- Add migrateWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 / migrateUserWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 called on sync

Migration: automatic on next workspace sync. No manual action required.

repository.name: allagents
repository.description: CLI tool for managing multi-repo AI agent workspaces with plugin synchronization across multiple AI clients.

commit.message:
feat: auto-wrap flat SKILL.md repos as skills (#232) (#234)

When a plugin has no skills/ subdirectory, scan the plugin root for
directories containing SKILL.md and treat each as a skill. This gives
compatibility with the npx skills ecosystem (e.g. vercel-labs/agent-skills)
without any schema changes.

The enabledSkills/disabledSkills schema continues to work unchanged
since the plugin is still tracked as a workspace plugin entry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: support installing skills individually (#228) (#231)

- Fix plugin install --skill when plugin already installed: detect
  'Plugin already exists' error and if --skill flags provided, skip
  the install step and proceed directly to enabling skills + sync

- Add top-level `allagents skills` command as shorthand alias for

  with `npx skills`

- Enhance `skills add` with --from/-f option: if skill not found in
  installed plugins, installs the specified plugin source first then
  enables the skill (mirrors `npx skills add <pkg> --skill <name>`)

Architectural invariant locked:
> The bridge main thread is the UI/STA thread. All window creation, COM operations, plugin loads, and IPlugView calls happen on this thread. Pipe reader is background thread only.

Validation complete:
✅ Shipped Debug bridge byte-matches rebuilt Release bridge
✅ load_ack succeeds; openEditor successfully calls attached() without deadlock
✅ OB-Xd editor incomplete UI confirmed as plugin-side (beyond minimal host scope)

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: minimal-music-keyboard
repository.description: Lightweight system-tray MIDI player for Windows 11 — WinUI3, MeltySynth, NAudio WASAPI, per-instrument SoundFont

commit.message:
fix: correct SqlMetaData constructor selection for datetime2/datetimeoffset/time TVP columns

The previous fix for #393 used `match name, dbType with` where `name`
is the column name (e.g. "CreatedAt"), not the SQL type name. This meant
the pattern | "datetime2", SqlDbType.DateTime2 -> ... would never fire
unless the column happened to be literally named "datetime2".

The correct fix is to match on `dbType` alone, which always contains the
actual SQL type. This ensures that all DateTime2, DateTimeOffset and Time
TVP columns use SqlMetaData(name, dbType, 0uy, scale) rather than
SqlMetaData(name, dbType, precision, scale), avoiding the ArgumentException
"Metadata for field 'X' did not match the original record's metadata"
when the precision byte doesn't equal what SQL Server expects.

Closes #393

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: FSharp.Data.SqlClient
repository.description: A set of F# Type Providers for statically typed access to MS SQL database

commit.message:
feat: add `export-audit` CLI command for wallet transaction CSV export (#7700)

Wallet needs to export transactions as a CSV for auditing, with optional
date range filtering and fiat conversion.

## Changes

### `cli.rs`
- New `ExportAuditArgs` struct with: `--output-file`, `--start-date`,
`--end-date`, `--conversion-rate`, `--currency` (default: `USD`)
- New `ExportAudit(ExportAuditArgs)` variant in `CliCommands`
- `parse_utc_datetime` helper accepting RFC3339 and `YYYY-MM-DD` /
`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` formats

### `commands.rs`
- `ExportAudit` handler: fetches all completed transactions, filters by
date range, calls `write_audit_to_csv_file`
- `write_audit_to_csv_file`: emits CSV with columns matching the spec —
transaction type is `Deposit` (inbound/coinbase) or `Withdraw`
(outbound); transaction hash derived from first kernel hash; amounts in
XTM (Minotari); optional fiat column using supplied rate

### `wallet_modes.rs`
- Added `ExportAudit` arm to the exhaustive command-match test

## Example

```bash
minotari_console_wallet export-audit \
  --output-file audit.csv \
  --start-date 2021-01-01 \
  --end-date 2021-12-31 \
  --conversion-rate 3130.0 \
  --currency USD
```

Output CSV columns:
```
ID, Transaction Hash, Status, Transaction Type, DateTime (UTC),
From Address, To Address, Amount, AmountTicker, Amount (USD),
Txn Fee, FeeTicker, Fee (USD)
```

commit.message:
fix: resolve 7 failing Android unit tests

- DriftDetector: remove off-by-one (+1) in consistentReadings count
- DriftDetectorTest: fix manyPoints test to produce ascending timestamps
  with ascending distances (was reversed, causing decreasing distances)
- CatenaryCalculatorTest: widen integration test bound from 8x to 9x depth
  to account for 20% safety margin on 7:1 scope ratio (~8.3x)
- GeoCalculationsTest: use 99.9m instead of exact 100.0m for boundary test
  to avoid float precision mismatch between destinationPoint and distanceMeters
- ProtocolMessageParser: add testOptions.unitTests.isReturnDefaultValues=true
  to build.gradle.kts so android.util.Log calls don't throw in unit tests

commit.message:
fix: address PR #47 review comments

- Fix shallow copy bug: clone answered object before mutation in handleAnswer
- Add CRLF to END:VCALENDAR for RFC 5545 compliance
- Wrap stopMonitoring DB/wear ops in try/finally to guarantee service stop
- Add comments explaining intentional useEffect dependency omissions
- Add CSS fallback comment for safe-area padding

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Filter ResizeObserver loop errors from dev server overlay (#30125)

The @rspack/dev-server overlay catches ResizeObserver loop errors as
runtime crashes, showing a full-screen error overlay that blocks
development. These errors are benign — they originate from
lit-virtualizer (used by ha-data-table) and simply mean not all
resize observations could be delivered in a single animation frame.

Add a client.overlay.runtimeErrors filter to the dev server config
that suppresses ResizeObserver loop messages while still surfacing
all real runtime errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix gallery integration card crash from invalid mock hassUrl (#30126)

The gallery mock set hass.auth.data.hassUrl to an empty string, which
caused brandsUrl() to throw when constructing a URL via new URL(base, '').
In production hassUrl is always a valid URL, so the mock now uses
location.origin to match real behavior.

Add iTipFooty.com.au auto-tipping integration

Automates submitting prediction engine picks to iTipFooty:

- Login via POST to services/login.php with session cookie management
- Parse tipping page HTML to extract game numbers, team names, form fields
- Map prediction engine picks (team IDs) to iTipFooty H/A selections
- Submit tips via POST to services/SubmitTips.php
- Handle locked games (already kicked off) gracefully

Backend:
- New service: backend/src/services/itipfooty.ts
- New route: /api/itipfooty/status and /api/itipfooty/submit
- Credentials loaded from .env (ITIPFOOTY_USERNAME, PASSWORD, COMP_ID)
- dotenv added for env var loading
- Submission logged to DataSourceLog

Frontend:
- Settings page: iTipFooty Integration card with status display
- Submit Tips button with detailed result feedback per game
- Shows configuration instructions when not configured

Security:
- Credentials stored in backend/.env (gitignored)
- .env.example provided with empty placeholders
- Status endpoint masks username with ***

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Centralize card motion presets and UI tokens, add reduced-motion support (#25)

* Initial plan

* refactor: centralize card motion presets and UI tokens, improve GPU friendliness

- Create lib/ui/tokens.ts with shared card sizing (2:3 aspect ratio),
  perspective, hover lift, reveal scale, and eliminated opacity constants
- Create lib/motion/presets.ts with spring (hover/fan/stack), flip
  (normal/instant), timing (fast/normal/dramatic), and reduced-motion
  (INSTANT) motion presets
- Refactor InteractiveCard, CardFanHand, CardStack to consume shared
  presets and tokens instead of inline duplicated configs
- Add useReducedMotion() from framer-motion for accessibility
- Add will-change: transform on actively animating layers
- Align card sizes to exact 2:3 ratio (56×84, 80×120, 112×168)
- Slightly tighten stack reveal from 0.6s to 0.5s via TIMING_DRAMATIC
- No public API, prop, or card engine logic changes

fix: Use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude (#3100)

* fix: use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude

The recipe runner and delegation scripts hardcoded 'claude' as the agent
binary, causing failures when amplihack is run with other agents (copilot,
codex, etc.).

Changes:
- cli.py: Set AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var at dispatch for all 5 agent cmds
- rust_runner.py: Forward --agent-binary flag to Rust recipe runner
- platform_cli.py: Read env var instead of hardcoding 'claude'
- delegate_response.py (3 mirrors): Read env var + add missing 'import os'

The Rust recipe runner companion changes are in
rysweet/amplihack-recipe-runner#29.

Fixes #3099

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review findings - empty string edge case and missed hardcoded files

- Fix orchestrator.py: use ${AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY:-claude} bash fallback
- Fix executor.py (2 locations): same bash fallback pattern for remote execution
- Fix empty string edge case: use 'or "claude"' pattern instead of default param
  in platform_cli.py and all 3 delegate_response.py mirrors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Ubuntu <azureuser@devr.yb0a3bvkdghunmsjr4s3fnfhra.phxx.internal.cloudapp.net>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add services/realtime, packages/ui, and apps/web

- services/realtime: WebSocket + SSE realtime server using Fastify, NATS,
  Redis, and JWT authentication. Supports channel subscriptions, typing
  indicators, and domain event fan-out.

- packages/ui: Tailwind-based React component library with Button, Input,
  Card, Badge, Spinner, Alert, Modal, Avatar, Sidebar, and DataTable
  components.

- apps/web: Next.js 14 App Router frontend with login, dashboard, courses,
  forums (with realtime post updates via WebSocket), grades, assignments,
  and messages pages.

- packages/sdk: Fix missing DOM lib in tsconfig causing fetch/RequestInit/
  URLSearchParams type errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 4 complete: full thesis first draft (5 chapters, ~12,000 words)

thesis/chapter1-introduction.md (~3,000 words):
- Motivation, research questions, chaos engineering background
- Kubernetes security model (PSA, NetworkPolicy, Falco)
- Literature review: Chaos Mesh, kube-bench, Gremlin, Trivy, gap analysis
- Ethical considerations

thesis/chapter2-methodology.md (~2,000 words):
- Design science research approach
- 5 Whys RCA motivating requirements
- MoSCoW requirements (Must/Should/Could/Won't)
- Technology selection rationale (Go, Kind, Calico, Falco, Helm)
- Experimental design (golden vs misconfigured cluster)
- Validity threats (internal, external, construct)

thesis/chapter3-architecture.md (~3,500 words):
- Full system architecture diagram
- All 5 internal packages documented
- PSA admission edge case implementation
- AlertStore thread-safety and ClearRule design
- MTTD calculation rationale
- Falco Helm chart compatibility issues and fixes
- 27-test testing strategy

thesis/chapter4-results.md (~2,500 words):
- RQ1: 100% detection, two control layers validated
- RQ2: ~4,200x faster than manual, reproducible
- RQ3: MTTD architecture sound; null in local runs (deployment constraint)
- Threats to validity: network-egress Kind limitation, sample size

thesis/chapter5-conclusion.md (~1,000 words):
- Contributions summary
- Limitations
- Future work: expanded experiments, cloud SIEM, parallel execution
- Reflection on chaos engineering applied to security

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve VST3 editor diagnostics

Preserve bridge-supplied VST3 editor diagnostics in the WinUI settings flow, use them for inline status and editor-unavailable dialogs, and stop re-enabling the editor button after unsupported/faulted states.
